pages.full_text:
Friday 18 April.
    We left at about 6.15 pm for Robert Grimley's installation at __Bristol__. Simon, Eve, John & Charles. We camped at [?Failand] ** a field with cows & a smell of silage. Very

[Photo: Clifton Suspension Bridge with hot air balloons, captioned "BRISTOL"]

[illegible caption below photo]
pages.notes: No handwritten page number visible in top-left corner. The campsite name after 'camped at' reads something like 'Failand' — could also be 'Failand' (a village near Bristol) or another reading. Two asterisks (**) follow the campsite name, possibly a rating. The photo shows the Clifton Suspension Bridge with hot air balloons; the printed caption reads 'BRISTOL' — this is a postcard or printed photo. There is some text below the photo that is mostly obscured/cut off in the scan. Text continues on right page ('Very ... peaceful'). 'Robert Grimley's installation' refers to an art or design installation. Year: April 18 fell on a Friday in 1997 (also 1986, 2003) — 1997 is the most probable year within the volume range given context, but cannot be confirmed from this page alone.

pages.full_text:
peaceful, except for John's & Charles' squabbling. Cold in the night.

__Sat 19th__
Charles & John argued while we had tea. A slight show. Then a dry, chilly [?ow] wind all day. We drove over the Clifton Suspension Bridge & zigzagged down to the [?docks]. We walked along to the SS Great Britain. We saw the Balmoral on the way.
    The SS Great Britain was designed in 1843 by Brunel. "The Concorde of the [?19]." Brunel realised that you didn't need proportionally more coal to drive proportionally bigger boats. A stupid captain beached her in Ireland on her 5th voyage. She sailed to Australia often & was finally abandoned in the Falklands. They appear to have run out of money for her renovation.
    We returned to the van & got dressed up for the cathedral. It took ages in 2 buses & slow traffic to get to Nicola's & Mark's for lunch. Delicious kiche & banoffie.
    Nicola drove us to the cathedral &
pages.notes: Handwritten page number '25' visible in top-right corner (partially cropped but readable). A '7' is visible at the left margin — this may be the handwritten page number from the left (facing) page showing through, or a marginal annotation. Text at top continues from left page ('...Very / peaceful'). The word after 'chilly' is unclear — possibly 'ow' or an abbreviation. '[?docks]' — the word reads as 'docks' (Bristol Docks / Floating Harbour). 'The Concorde of the [?19]' — the quote appears to reference '19th century' but the end of the phrase is unclear. 'kiche' is likely a misspelling of 'quiche'. 'banoffie' is banoffee pie. 'Balmoral' is likely the paddle steamer MV Balmoral. 'Falklands' — the SS Great Britain was indeed abandoned in the Falkland Islands; the diary's account is historically accurate. Text cuts off at bottom — continues on next spread. Year estimated as 1997 (April 19 = Saturday); see left page notes.
